title: The Best Lower Face Botox Injection Depths | Botox Injection Depth Guide
filename: The Best Lower Face Botox Injection Depths  ｜ Botox Injection Depth Guide.en.vtt
excerpt: Some of the strangest side effects that you get from botulin toxin treatments are about injecting at the wrong depth if you understand how the depth of your botuline toxin injections affect the result you can interpret and solve side effects way better let's start by looking at t…

segments.text: some of the strangest side effects that you get from botulin toxin treatments are about injecting at the wrong depth if you understand how the depth of your botuline toxin injections affect the result you can interpret and solve side effects way better let's start by looking at the master muscle so that you can relate what you just saw on that patient with how the injection depth may have caused that result and the important thing with understanding depth is that it is a relative term so it's relative to the total size of the structures involved so with the master muscle if I say deep injection I probably mean having the needle tip in the lower third of the muscle if that's a really big muscle that's actually a different depth to if it's a really small muscle and this is something that you need to figure out by examining your patient every procedure has to be based on the patient in front of you not on some academic idea of what the right millimeter by millimeter depth is so whenever you treat a patient with a master muscle that needs a little bit of shrinking I always like to examine them first and get them to bite down and reveal the size and the thickness of

their muscle as it's Contracting you can do this simply by doing this I think it's quite easy to get an idea of how thick that muscle is and then aim for placing that muscle that you're least likely to cause the side effects why do people generally inject deeper with the master muscle it's because on the surface of the master muscle you have the rosaurus the resource literally originates from the surface of the muscle and it plugs obviously into the modiolus here and is part of your smile if you relax the rosarius muscle you get the oval side effect of not being able to smile properly so we want to hide our botronym toxin underneath the origin of the rosaurus muscle but we also want to relax as much of the master as possible if you are very deep and there's a lot of Contracting muscle on top of the master then you end up with the strange side effect of a bulge as the surface fibers contract in an odd way without the balance normally created by the deeper fibers so you get this pinching bulge that causes a protrusion my colleague just asked does it mean that

if you have a thinner muscle you actually have to be deeper and this is actually correct if you think about what we're actually trying to do in terms of safety we're trying to avoid the surface of the muscle while also trying to relax the bulk of the muscle so there's a compromise going on but a thinner muscle you have to be further away from the surface or as far away as possible which means in relation to the periosteum you are closer it would be more appropriate to be nearer the bone in a very thin muscle than if you have a very big muscle where you may be further away this is what I mean about the depth being relative let's talk about the Dao this is one of the most popular lower face treatments we treat the Dao in order to allow a lift of the oral commission make you look happier even if you're not so this muscle is involved in pulling on the modiolis and depressing the mouth so if you relax it you get the opposite now the downside is many clinicians hit the muscle next to it and also deep to it which is the depressor labia is an important muscle

with smiling when you smile a true genuine broad smile you actually pull your lower lip down and out and if you want to understand what this muscle looks like in extreme situations you can I often notice people's anatomy and I always remember Cherie Blair having a really strong depressor labia eye muscle and you can see what that muscle does then you can imagine what will happen if you relax that muscle which is as you smile your lip on one side typically does not pull down and you get an asymmetrical smile very upsetting because every time you get happy people think there's something wrong with your face think often people react as if you've had a stroke it looks like something's wrong and this makes you not want to smile so this is why clinicians hate this side effect and it happens because of a combination of the depth and the position of the injection point that we are using to relax the depressor angularis Oris and how that sometimes affects the depressor labia depth is the first one we can talk about as you're sliding the needle in to inject the depressor angularosaurus if you go deep enough you are likely to get closer to

the fibers that are actually pulling on the lip medially from the depressor labia there's also another way that you can reduce the risk of hitting this muscle which is to move more Superior I find it intuitively safer to not inject on the jawline this is one of the common injection points clinicians use you're trying to find the yellow which does this find the origin of it and inject here the problem with this if you inject deeply is that you're very close to the overlapping fibers with the depressive labi and I think they can diffuse more easily here and affect the lip if you were to slide up and feel the lateral border of the depressor angularis Oris which you can do if your patient contracts like this you can actually feel where that muscle is so I try and be superior and lateral so I'm as far away from the lip depressors as possible because the only other muscle here is a little bit of the buccinator muscle and the rosaurus potentially but although that's usually more Superior so we're trying to find this space in between where you're far

enough away from all of those key muscles and you can relax the down Pull without relaxing of the any of the other vectors in the face so let's have a look at a couple of strange side effects that you can cause with injection depth error and there's also another cause which I'll talk about towards the end so let's have a look at this one sometimes called the kidney bean chin so this happens when you're treating the mentalis muscle or obviously treat that to prevent the chin being pulled upwards sometimes and it also removes some of the texture on the skin that you get when those muscle fibers are continuously contracted you get little indentations so you can inject the mentalis muscle relax those and usually get a good aesthetic result what often happens however is that you do don't treat the complete depth of the muscle so this can happen because clinicians are trying to not inject too deeply or they're trying to make sure that they're at the right depth we're obviously concerned with hitting the depressor labii muscle here too which is also a reason why many clinicians will stay more superficial and what you can see in this video is the exact result of

staying superficial if you look closely at her chin you can see that the top of her chin is not moving and underneath you can see some strands of the mentalis muscle that are pulling up causing this bunching on the surface of the skin which looks like a kidney beam so little bunching caused by muscle underneath pulling and running into the relaxed muscle on top so this side effect is caused by injecting two superficially but there's also another cause or an easy Pitfall that new injectors often make which is if you see this on day two and three you start to panic and you think you've got a side effect but if you wait until day six or seven often it resolves and really what's happening here is there's a period of time where some of the muscle fibers are reacting sooner to the botulinum toxin than the others and so there's a natural imbalance this is true with all the treatments that we have there's a period where it's Half Baked and you need to hold your nerve and let your patient go through at least until a week and actually technically it's two weeks but I think usually you can tell by day eight nine what the end result is going

to look like if I was to fix this particular case all I would do is look for where those muscle fibers are still Contracting and directly treat them and what I can see in this case is you can see the indentation on her chin where the muscle fibes are pulling in I would go straight into those parallel with the muscle aiming for its origin which is near the mental crease and I would inject two units either side and that would probably solve it within about three to five days in this next case you see a problem caused by the exact opposite problem this is also a mentalis treatment but it resulted in a different side effect the side effect here is that the depressor labia eye muscle has been affected the depressive ABI is normally pulling the lip outward and natural and that's now been relaxed while her chin is still actually Contracting so what has occurred is that the needle has probably gone underneath the muscle and maybe a little bit lateral to the muscle and affected the depressor labia muscle instead of the mentalis this is what we're trying to avoid by injecting at the right depth and these two cases really illustrate the difficulty and why Precision is important because there is so often a balancing act going on in the

lower face between too deep and too superficial and we need to really have a clear understanding of how these muscles work where the origin of the muscle is where the attachment is and how to accurately stay within that muscle and not affect the nearby muscles which is why I prefer a technique that's a bit more like this so I actually think the mentalis muscle is quite hard to understand in three dimensions until you think about it in a different way most textbooks give you this idea that it's a muscle that just runs around your chin but it's better to think of it as a muscle that's almost projecting out of your chin and then is meeting on the skin surface so its origin is near the mental crease and it's projecting outwards and I'll exaggerate it with this piece of blue tack which is not white tack if I place it here where its origin is what I'd like you to imagine is instead of it being flat against the muscle that it actually projects out like this so this is the reason why as it gets more active it pulls your chin inwards but it also helps you decide a better

way of injecting it because I think if you come at it like this you will go straight underneath it quite a lot of the time and merge out the other side and this is where you might be closer to the depressor labia muscle similarly if you're trying to be superficial it's easier to relax just this part of it and have fibers underneath are still Contracting so my preferred way to treat it and especially if you have a side effect and you're trying to correct the side effect is to think about it this way and then to come in a way that you think is parallel with those Mentalist fibers so I inject it like that and if you're accurately in the bulk of the muscle that gives you the most chance of treating the whole muscle without treating the nearby muscles so here's a confusing question is this a deep or a superficial injection the truth is this is really about just accurately injecting the active muscle so we are entering superficially and it doesn't really matter if you go deeper or not as long as you're using a normal insulin syringe because you're in the channel of the muscle the whole way so

you're basically aiming to be in the middle depth layer because I don't want to be underneath on the periosteum which is a risk for depressed labii and I don't want to be too superficial because then I'm going to only relax the surface of the muscle and get the kidney bean problem so I'm actually at an intermediate depth when you consider the plane from the surface of the chin the anterior side down but if you think of about it in terms of injecting the muscle I'm just trying to stay in the middle of the muscle so I hope that helped you understand injection depth in the lower face don't forget not to blame your injections in the first two to three days especially because those treatments are Half Baked give them a chance to play out and it may turn out better than you think if you want to learn more about botuline toxin in the upper face I have a great download at the bottom with this video and you can also watch this video here where I talk about the injection depths in the upper face
